reasoning: The probability of Ukraine joining NATO before July 2025 is near zero. First, on February 24, 2025, U.S. National Security Advisor Mike Waltz explicitly stated that Ukrainian membership in NATO is 'not back on the table.' Given that NATO accession requires unanimous consent from all 32 members, opposition from the U.S. administration makes an invitation impossible. Second, even if an invitation were miraculously offered today, the accession process requires ratification by all 32 national parliaments. The fastest accession in NATO's history (Finland) took 11 months, and Sweden's took much longer due to delays by Hungary and Turkey. With only 3.5 months until the June 30 deadline, there is simply not enough time to complete this process. Finally, Polymarket traders priced this at 3% in early March, reflecting the sheer impossibility of overcoming the political and procedural hurdles in such a short timeframe.
